commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=876ca57732db79a5673e34a5b059395ee2abbe36)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds canonical `title` support across gateway session schemas, create/list/patch/resolve, stored `SessionEntry` metadata, `/name`, Control UI display types, reserved plugin slot keys, and focused tests while mirroring legacy `label`.

PR surface: Source +84, Tests +35. Total +119 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding by source inspection: PR head accepts nullable `title` and `label` patch fields, but mixed null/string pairs skip mismatch rejection and choose one side. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review remains needed because the permanent title-versus-label contract and contributor real behavior proof are blockers that an automated repair lane cannot fully resolve.

target.title: fix(sessions): migrate session naming to canonical title
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Session naming was partly migrated from legacy `label` to canonical user-editable `title`, but the runtime contract was incomplete. New gateway schemas accepted `title` while `sessions.create` dropped it, `sessions.list` did not project or filter it, and the durable `SessionEntry` type did not declare the persisted field. That left new title-based clients and older label-based clients able to disagree about the same session name.

Additionally, `sessions.patch({ label: null })` and the new `sessions.patch({ title: null })` clear paths were broken: `normalizeSessionTitleText()` was called on `null` before the null-clear branch, causing `null.trim()` to throw.

## Why This Change Was Made

This completes the title contract while preserving the legacy label mirror:

- `sessions.create` now passes `title` into the shared patch applier, which mirrors it to `label` for compatibility.
- `SessionEntry` now declares `title?: string` as durable session metadata.
- Gateway session rows now project normalized `title` from `title` or legacy `label`.
- `sessions.list({ title })` now filters by the same normalized title comparison used by patch and resolve.
- Session list search includes the canonical title field.
- Nullable `title: null` and `label: null` clears are preserved as sentinels before string normalization, fixing the regression where `null` reached `normalizeSessionTitleText()`.
- Focused tests cover `/name`, create, patch, resolve, list projection/filtering, legacy label compatibility, and nullable clear regression cases.

## User Impact

Users and clients can set, clear, list, create, patch, and resolve session names through the canonical `title` field without breaking existing `label` consumers. Legacy label-only session entries still appear as titled rows and remain resolvable through title-based lookup. Session name clears via `sessions.patch({ label: null })` and `sessions.patch({ title: null })` now work correctly.

## Evidence

Head SHA: `876ca57732`

### ClawSweeper P1 finding fixed: nullable clear regression

**Bug:** `src/gateway/sessions-patch.ts:362-363` called `normalizeSessionTitleText(patch.title)` / `normalizeSessionTitleText(patch.label)` whenever the field was not `undefined`, including when it was `null`. This caused `null.trim()` to throw before the `raw === null` clear branch could run, breaking both existing `sessions.patch({ label: null })` clients and the new `sessions.patch({ title: null })` path.

**Fix:** Check for `null` explicitly before normalizing. `null` is passed through as a sentinel so the existing clear branch fires correctly. String values are still normalized with `trim().normalize("NFC")` as before.

```diff
- const rawTitle = patch.title !== undefined ? normalizeSessionTitleText(patch.title) : undefined;
- const rawLabel = patch.label !== undefined ? normalizeSessionTitleText(patch.label) : undefined;
+ const rawTitle =
+   patch.title === undefined
+     ? undefined
+     : patch.title === null
+       ? null
+       : normalizeSessionTitleText(patch.title);
+ const rawLabel =
+   patch.label === undefined
+     ? undefined
+     : patch.label === null
+       ? null
+       : normalizeSessionTitleText(patch.label);
```

### After-fix: nullable clear regression tests

Three new tests added to `src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts`:

```
✓ clears title and label when patch sets title: null
✓ clears title and label when patch sets label: null
✓ clears title and label when both title: null and label: null
```

Each test creates a session with both `title` and `label` set, patches with the null clear, and asserts both fields are `undefined` after the patch.

### After-fix: all touched tests pass on new head

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-name.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts
```

Result:

```
Test Files  14 passed (14)
Tests  718 passed (718)
Duration  58.20s

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  7 passed (7)
Duration  50.42s

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 124.47s
```

### Previous evidence (still valid)

Head SHA: `135983cebf`

#### Lint gate passes on previous head

The previous head (`6d05bb66ac`) failed `check-lint` with:
```
src/plugins/session-entry-slot-keys.ts(141,15): error TS2344: Type '"title"' does not satisfy the constraint 'never'.
```

This was fixed by adding `"title"` to `SESSION_ENTRY_RESERVED_SLOT_KEY_LIST` in `src/plugins/session-entry-slot-keys.ts`.

#### ClawSweeper P2 findings addressed

1. **sessions-patch.ts: compare title/label after parsing** — Raw `title` and `label` strings are now normalized with `trim().normalize("NFC")` before comparison.
2. **session-display.ts: add title to shared UI row contract** — `GatewaySessionRow` in `ui/src/ui/types.ts` now declares `title?: string`.

@clawsweeper re-review
target.author: jason-allen-oneal

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
